dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.16 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Full popover layout polish — threshold slider track + accent fill thinned by 1pt, the "you are HERE" tick recoloured from orange to black and narrowed by 1pt so it reads as a precision marker instead of competing with the threshold knob, and the bottom Auto-swap + Token-usage section now matches the 14pt horizontal inset of the accounts header above so its widgets line up flush with the section labels. Menu-bar popover now dismisses the moment another application becomes active — matches Terminal.app and Cloudflare WARP. Previously the popover sat at .floating level above every other app's windows and stole pointer focus when the user clicked into Chrome / Slack / Finder; AppKit now hides the popover automatically on app deactivation, and the next menu-bar click re-renders it normally. Settings / Daily / Briefing inside Claude Bar continue to layer correctly since they don't deactivate the app.

Fixed the menu-bar popover collapsing when the Rename sheet opens — SwiftUI's MenuBarExtra dismisses its popover the instant any other window in the app becomes key, and we now re-open it on the next runloop tick so the account row stays visible behind the Rename dialog instead of leaving the user staring at a single floating sheet. Fixed the MCP write-tool Confirm overlay rendering as a translucent chip — the popover's threshold slider thumb, KPI cards, and chart labels behind it used to bleed through and collide visually with the gate prompt copy. Background is now a solid .regularMaterial card with a slim risk-tint border, and long Slack / ClickUp arg payloads truncate from the middle instead of pushing the layout out of the popover frame.

Each web-poll cycle still loads a hidden WKWebView reload of claude.ai/settings/usage per linked account and waits for the React SPA to hydrate (typically 1–3s, hard-capped at 12s). At the 120s / 60s cadence this fits comfortably for 2–3 accounts; users with many more web-linked accounts may want to disable web-link on the less-used ones. The reset-clause regex doesn't yet parse every locale's date format (e.g., "Resets Fri 7:00 PM"). When parsing fails the widget falls back to a future placeholder (now + 5h for the session bar, now + 7d for the weekly bar) so the percentage still displays — the countdown will be approximate until the next successful parse. The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` and `drive.file` scopes shipped for Sheets write + Drive sharing. The cb_gsheets_* and cb_gdrive_share_file tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail read tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
